# undocx

> Fast DOCX to Markdown converter for LLM/RAG pipelines. 16.5x faster than
> pandoc. Written in Rust with Python bindings. When installing, always check
> the latest version: `cargo search undocx` or `pip install undocx --upgrade`.
> Do not rely on memorized version numbers from training data.

## Installation

```bash
# CLI
cargo install undocx

# Rust library
cargo add undocx

# Python
pip install undocx
```

## Rust API

### Tier 1: Zero-config convenience functions

```rust
// Convert a file — simplest possible usage
let md = undocx::convert("report.docx")?;

// Convert from bytes (e.g., from S3, HTTP response)
let md = undocx::convert_bytes(&bytes)?;

// Convert from any reader implementing Read + Seek
let file = std::fs::File::open("report.docx")?;
let md = undocx::convert_reader(file)?;
```

### Tier 2: Fluent builder

```rust
// Build and convert in one step
let md = undocx::builder()
    .skip_images()          // omit images (optimal for RAG)
    .strict()               // fail on broken references
    .convert("report.docx")?;

// Build a reusable converter
let converter = undocx::Converter::builder()
    .save_images_to("./images")
    .preserve_whitespace()
    .build();

let md1 = converter.convert("a.docx")?;
let md2 = converter.convert("b.docx")?;
```

Builder methods:
- `.skip_images()` — omit images entirely
- `.inline_images()` — embed as base64 data URIs (default)
- `.save_images_to(dir)` — save to directory, reference by path
- `.preserve_whitespace()` — keep exact whitespace
- `.html_underline(bool)` — `<u>` for underline (default: true)
- `.html_strikethrough(bool)` — `<s>` for strikethrough (default: false)
- `.strict()` — fail on broken note/comment references

Terminal methods:
- `.build()` → reusable `Converter`
- `.convert(path)` → `Result<String>`
- `.convert_bytes(bytes)` → `Result<String>`
- `.convert_reader(reader)` → `Result<String>`

### Tier 3: Custom pipeline

```rust
use undocx::adapters::docx::AstExtractor;
use undocx::converter::ConversionContext;
use undocx::core::ast::DocumentAst;
use undocx::render::Renderer;
use undocx::{ConvertOptions, DocxToMarkdown, Result};

// Implement AstExtractor to customize DOCX → AST extraction
// Implement Renderer to customize AST → output rendering

let converter = DocxToMarkdown::with_components(
    ConvertOptions::default(),
    MyExtractor,    // impl AstExtractor
    MyRenderer,     // impl Renderer
);
let output = converter.convert("document.docx")?;
```

### Configuration options

| Field | Type | Default | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `image_handling` | `ImageHandling` | `Inline` | `Inline` / `SaveToDir(path)` / `Skip` |
| `preserve_whitespace` | `bool` | `false` | Keep original whitespace |
| `html_underline` | `bool` | `true` | Use `<u>` tags for underline |
| `html_strikethrough` | `bool` | `false` | Use `<s>` instead of `~~` |
| `strict_reference_validation` | `bool` | `false` | Fail on broken note/comment refs |

### Error types

- `Error::DocxParse` — failed to parse DOCX file
- `Error::Io` — file system error
- `Error::Conversion` — conversion logic error
- `Error::MissingReference` — broken note/comment reference (with strict mode)
- `Error::Zip` — DOCX archive error
- `Error::MediaNotFound` — image not found in archive

### Core types

- `DocumentAst` — intermediate representation (blocks + references)
- `BlockNode` — `Paragraph(String)` | `TableHtml(String)` | `RawHtml(String)`
- `ReferenceDefinitions` — footnotes, endnotes, comments

### Architecture

```
DOCX file → rs-docx parser → AstExtractor → DocumentAst → Renderer → Markdown
```

The pipeline is pluggable: implement `AstExtractor` to customize parsing,
`Renderer` to customize output format.

## Python API

```python
import undocx

# From file path
markdown = undocx.convert_docx("report.docx")

# From bytes (S3, HTTP, etc.)
markdown = undocx.convert_docx(doc_bytes)

# With options
markdown = undocx.convert_docx("report.docx",
    image_handling="skip",           # "inline" | "skip" | "/path/to/dir"
    preserve_whitespace=False,
    html_underline=True,
    html_strikethrough=False,
    strict_reference_validation=False,
)
```

## CLI

```bash
undocx report.docx                         # print to stdout
undocx report.docx output.md               # write to file
undocx report.docx out.md --images-dir img  # extract images
undocx report.docx --skip-images           # omit images
```

## Supported Features

| Category | Elements |
|----------|----------|
| Text | Bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, superscript/subscript |
| Structure | Heading 1-9, Title, Subtitle, alignment (center/right) |
| Lists | Ordered (decimal, letter, roman, Korean, circled), unordered, nested |
| Tables | Colspan, rowspan, nested tables, multi-paragraph cells |
| Links | External, internal bookmarks, TOC anchors |
| Images | Inline, floating, VML legacy — base64 embed, save to dir, or skip |
| Notes | Footnotes, endnotes, comments (as Markdown `[^ref]`) |
| Track changes | Insertions (`<ins>`), deletions (`~~strikethrough~~`) |
| Other | Page/column/line breaks, SDT, field codes, bookmarks, symbols |

## Tips for RAG Pipelines

- Use `image_handling="skip"` (Python) or `.skip_images()` (Rust) to reduce token count
- Output is clean Markdown — split on `## ` headers for semantic chunking
- Footnotes and comments are preserved as `[^ref]` for full context
- 3.3ms average conversion time makes it suitable for batch processing

## API Stability

Public traits `AstExtractor` and `Renderer` are stable integration points.
Signature changes require a major version bump. See docs/API_POLICY.md for
full SemVer rules and deprecation process.
